steps.title: I–V Characteristics
steps.content:
An **I–V graph** shows how current (I) varies with voltage (V) for different components:

**Resistor (ohmic):** Straight line through origin — constant resistance (obeys Ohm's Law)

**Filament lamp:** Curved line — resistance increases as temperature rises

**Diode:** Current only flows in one direction (forward bias). Very high resistance in reverse.

steps.title: I–V Characteristics
steps.question: A filament lamp's I–V graph curves — it is not a straight line. What does this tell you about its resistance?
options: Its resistance increases as the voltage increases
Its resistance decreases as current increases
It does not obey any electrical laws
steps.correctFeedback: Correct! The curved I–V graph shows that the gradient (I/V) decreases — so V/I (resistance) increases. This is because the filament heats up and resistance rises with temperature.
steps.misconceptionFeedback: For an ohmic resistor, R = V/I is constant (straight I–V line). The lamp's curve means the ratio V/I changes — resistance increases at higher temperatures. This is a non-ohmic component.
